hero.headline: Chandigarh Online MBA in Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Harvard and PwC certification options

sections.tldr: Chandigarh Online MBA in Operations Management covers production planning, AI-Predictive Supply Chain, Digital Twins, and ESG Procurement, PwC-taught modules (premium tier), zero-cost direct university EMI, and 25% Early Bird scholarship at Rs 1,65,000. NIRF #19, NAAC A+. For manufacturing, supply chain, and operations careers.

about.body:
Operations Management is among the most employment-stable MBA specialisations in India, driven by the country's manufacturing growth under PLI schemes, e-commerce logistics expansion, and industrial digitisation programmes. Chandigarh University Online's Operations Management MBA reflects the modern operations landscape with AI-Predictive Supply Chain, Digital Twins, and ESG Procurement subjects alongside core production planning and quantitative methods.

The programme is designed for working professionals in manufacturing, e-commerce logistics, industrial engineering, and operations consulting who want to formalise their management credentials while continuing to work.

For Tier 2 enrollees, PwC's Operations Consulting and Supply Chain advisory practice brings live frameworks in operational transformation, supply chain risk, and digital operations into the co-taught modules.

Chandigarh University holds NIRF #19 overall and NIRF #32 in Management 2025, NAAC A+ accredited. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all Chandigarh MBA specialisations. See the main Chandigarh Online MBA page for core subject list.

subjects.name: Strategy, Business and Globalization
subjects.desc: Operations strategy alignment with corporate goals in global manufacturing and service environments.

subjects.name: Decision Science
subjects.desc: Quantitative techniques - linear programming, queuing models, and simulation for operations problems.

subjects.name: Production Planning and Control
subjects.desc: MRP, MRPII, capacity planning, master production scheduling, and shop floor control.

subjects.name: Quantitative Techniques and Methods
subjects.desc: Operations research tools: transportation models, assignment problems, inventory optimisation.

subjects.name: Operations Management and Strategy
subjects.desc: Process design, lean operations, Six Sigma frameworks, and competitive operations strategy.

subjects.name: Logistics and Supply Chain Management
subjects.desc: Supply chain network design, inventory positioning, transportation modes, and 3PL management.

subjects.name: Project Management
subjects.desc: Project delivery for operations improvement, capex projects, and manufacturing facility launches.

subjects.name: AI for Managers
subjects.desc: AI applications in demand sensing, production scheduling optimisation, and quality prediction.

subjects.name: AI-Predictive Supply Chain and Digital Twins
subjects.desc: ML-driven demand forecasting, digital twin technology for factory and supply chain simulation.

subjects.name: Sustainable Supply Chain and ESG Procurement
subjects.desc: Green procurement standards, Scope 3 emissions measurement, and ESG supplier evaluation frameworks.

subjects.name: Capstone Project II
subjects.desc: Applied operations improvement project on a real manufacturing or logistics challenge.

whoHires.body:
Operations Management MBA graduates from Chandigarh University Online are hired across manufacturing and supply chain industries:

**Industries:** Automotive, FMCG manufacturing, e-commerce logistics, electronics manufacturing, pharma, steel, textiles, and engineering companies

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Analyst, Supply Chain Coordinator, Production Planner, Procurement Executive, Logistics Manager (junior)

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Operations Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Plant Manager, Procurement Manager, Lean Manufacturing Lead

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, Head of Supply Chain, COO, Director of Manufacturing, VP Procurement

**Top hiring organisations:** Maruti Suzuki, Honda India, Tata Motors, L&T, Larsen and Toubro, Amazon Logistics, Flipkart Supply Chain, ITC Manufacturing, HUL Supply Chain, and PLI scheme manufacturers in electronics, pharma, and specialty chemicals

skills.body:
The Operations Management specialisation builds skills that manufacturing and supply chain employers test at manager and plant head levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Production Planning: MRP and ERP implementation, capacity requirement planning, and inventory control systems
- Quantitative Operations: linear programming for plant allocation, queue management models, and simulation-based bottleneck analysis
- Supply Chain: network optimisation, safety stock calculation, vendor managed inventory, and 3PL performance management
- Digital Operations: digital twin conceptual application, predictive maintenance AI models, and AI-driven demand forecasting

**Applied skills:**
- Lean and Six Sigma: waste elimination using VSM, DMAIC project management, and process capability improvement
- ESG Procurement: supplier sustainability auditing, Scope 3 measurement, and responsible sourcing compliance documentation

**Soft skills:** Communicating operational KPIs and improvement proposals to senior management, managing cross-functional teams on operational change projects, handling supplier negotiation under cost and quality pressure, and building operational resilience plans for supply chain disruptions.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Chandigarh Online MBA Operations Management against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA Production and Operations:** Amity's programme covers operations fundamentals well at NIRF #22. CU's curriculum includes AI-Predictive Supply Chain, Digital Twins, and ESG Procurement that Amity's older syllabus may not reflect. CU Tier 2 adds PwC Operations Consulting content. Fee: CU Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 vs Amity Rs 2,25,000.

**vs Manipal Jaipur Online MBA Operations:** Both are NAAC A+ with similar fee profiles. CU differentiates through NIRF #19 (stronger rank), Digital Twin and ESG Procurement subjects, and the PwC Operations advisory partnership (Tier 2). For manufacturing professionals in North India, CU's regional employer network is also an advantage.

**vs LPU Online MBA Operations:** LPU has NAAC A++ and lower fee. CU's advantage is NIRF #19, PwC consulting content, Harvard certificate, and the more current AI-forward operations syllabus. For professionals targeting senior manufacturing or supply chain roles where credentials are checked, CU's profile is stronger.

fees.body:
The Chandigarh University Online MBA in Operations Management offers 2 program tiers based on certification bundle:

**Tier 1 (Primary) - Standard with Harvard ManageMentor Certificate**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000
- 25% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,65,000 net payable (Rs 55,000 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 82,500 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 41,250 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,65,000 one-time
- Included certification: Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Tier 2 (Premium Upgrade) - 3-Certificate Bundle**:
- Actual program fee: Rs 2,20,000 (same base fee)
- 18% Early Bird Scholarship: Rs 1,80,400 net payable (Rs 39,600 savings)
- Payment options:
  - Annual: Rs 90,200 per year x 2 years
  - Semester-wise: Rs 45,100 per semester x 4 semesters
  - Full upfront: Rs 1,80,400 one-time
- Included certifications (3):
  1. PwC co-created and co-taught MBA modules (unmatched in India)
  2. CAPM (Certified Associate in Project Management) certification preparation
  3. Harvard Business Publishing certificate

**Additional charges (both tiers)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Chandigarh University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary, a differentiator among Indian online MBAs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options (6, 12, 18, or 24 months).

**Early Bird Scholarship terms**: Availability varies by admission cycle. The 25% Tier 1 scholarship and 18% Tier 2 scholarship are currently active but subject to timing. Reconfirm eligibility with our counsellor before enrolment.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Chandigarh University each quarter. Please reconfirm current tier options, Early Bird eligibility, and fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Chandigarh University MBA degree certificate with Operations Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 standards. NIRF #19 and NAAC A+ credentials are recognised by manufacturing, logistics, and operations employers.

Tier 1 graduates receive the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 graduates additionally receive PwC Operations Consulting co-teaching acknowledgement and CAPM certification preparation, strengthening profiles for operations consulting, capex project management, and supply chain transformation roles. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Chandigarh University Online MBA Operations Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Vikram S.
items.liked: AI-Predictive Supply Chain and Digital Twins subjects are ahead of what I expected from an MBA
items.disliked: Operations Research mathematics was challenging without engineering background
items.body: I was a production supervisor at an automotive components plant looking to move into management. The AI-Predictive Supply Chain content was directly applicable to the demand sensing project my employer was running. PwC Operations Consulting modules (Tier 2) gave me the transformation language to lead our plant digitalisation project. Got promoted to Operations Manager 3 months after completing Year 2.

items.name: Manpreet K.
items.liked: CAPM preparation alongside the MBA covered project management theory I needed
items.disliked: Lean and Six Sigma needed more hands-on simulation exercises
items.body: Chose Tier 2 for the CAPM and PwC combination. The CAPM preparation covered everything needed for the PMI examination. Operations Management and Strategy content was practical for my plant management role. Harvard Business Publishing certificate on my resume improved shortlisting rate significantly. NAAC A+ and NIRF #19 from CU are recognised by Ludhiana's manufacturing sector.

items.name: Sunita R.
items.liked: ESG Procurement subject directly relevant for our sustainability reporting requirements
items.disliked: LMS for collaborative capstone project work needs improvement
items.body: The ESG Procurement content was timely - my employer is under Scope 3 disclosure requirements and the supplier sustainability audit framework I learned was immediately applicable. Zero-cost EMI through CU made the Rs 1,65,000 Tier 1 very manageable. Good programme for operations professionals in supply chain and procurement roles.

faqs.question: What is the PwC partnership in Chandigarh Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Chandigarh University's premium tier includes full MBA modules co-created and co-taught by PwC industry experts. This is unmatched among Indian online MBAs - most offer only generic curriculum. PwC-taught modules are particularly valuable for Operations professionals seeking roles at consulting firms, Big 4 supply chain advisory, or operational transformation practices at large manufacturing companies. The PwC partnership is included in Tier 2 (premium) at Rs 1,80,400 net.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 fee options?
faqs.answer: Both tiers share the same Rs 2,20,000 base fee and identical curriculum. Tier 1 applies a 25% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,65,000 net with the Harvard Business Publishing certificate. Tier 2 applies an 18% Early Bird Scholarship at Rs 1,80,400 net with three certifications: Harvard, CAPM preparation, and PwC co-taught modules. For operations professionals targeting consulting or capex project management, the CAPM and PwC combination in Tier 2 adds strong value.

faqs.question: Is zero-cost EMI really available directly through Chandigarh University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chandigarh University offers zero-cost EMI through the university's own payment infrastructure, not through an NBFC or external marketing partner. Tenures of 6, 12, 18, and 24 months are typically available. Confirm current tenure options with our counsellor before enrolment.

faqs.question: Does the Operations MBA cover Six Sigma or Lean Manufacturing?
faqs.answer: Yes. Operations Management and Strategy in Sem 3 covers lean principles, waste reduction using VSM, and Six Sigma DMAIC framework. The Capstone Project II in Sem 4 is often applied to lean or process improvement problems. However, the MBA gives conceptual and application-level coverage rather than full Six Sigma belt certification. Candidates wanting Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt credentials should pursue dedicated IASSC or ASQ certification alongside the degree.

faqs.question: How does the ESG Procurement subject help in current roles?
faqs.answer: Sustainable Supply Chain and ESG Procurement in Sem 4 covers supplier sustainability auditing, Scope 3 emissions quantification, and green procurement policy design. As SEBI's BRSR (Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting) requirements expand to more Indian companies, procurement and operations managers are expected to manage ESG in their supplier base. This subject gives you the practical tools to lead these programmes within your organisation.

chooseIf: You work in manufacturing, logistics, or supply chain and want a NIRF #19 NAAC A+ credential at Rs 1,65,000.
You want PwC Operations Consulting content and CAPM preparation (Tier 2) alongside your MBA.
You are targeting AI-enabled operations, Digital Twins, or ESG supply chain roles.
You prefer zero-cost EMI directly through the university.
consider: You need NMIMS or IIM brand specifically for consulting firm or MNC operations hiring.
Your budget is under Rs 1.2 lakh and LPU NAAC A++ at lower fee is acceptable.
You want a dedicated supply chain certification (APICS CPIM/CSCP) alongside a lighter MBA.
